races.name: Tohoku Food Marathon 2026
races.description: A festive marathon for both runners and non-runners alike! Enjoy the famous local cuisine of the Tohoku region, savor the brewing water used for Japanese sake, and run along the beautiful lakeside in this fan run event in the coming spring. After crossing the finish line, indulge in a festival featuring specialty dishes from across Tohoku and delightful Japanese sake. Eat, drink, run, and have a great time experiencing the best of Tohoku!

Event/Eligibility.Full Marathon（Start time 8:30）: Age 16~ *Male and female who are healthy and able to complete the race Time Limit：6hours 30minutes
Event/Eligibility.Half Marathon（Start time 9:00-9:30）: Age 16~ *Male and female who are healthy and able to complete the race Time Limit：3hours 30minutes
Event/Eligibility.Half Marathon（Start time 9:30-10:00）: Age 16~ *Male and female who are healthy and able to complete the race Time Limit：3hours 30minutes
Event/Eligibility.Half Marathon（Start time 10:00-10:30）: Age 16~ *Male and female who are healthy and able to complete the race Time Limit：3hours 30minutes
Event/Eligibility.Half Marathon（Start time 10:30-11:00）: Age 16~ *Male and female who are healthy and able to complete the race Time Limit：3hours 30minutes
Event/Eligibility.5km run: Age 13~ *Male and female who are healthy and able to complete the race Time Limit：1hour
Event/Eligibility.Parent-child run (500m): Parents over 18 years old and children under age12 Time Limit：20 minutes

info.Start Time: 8:30(Full Marathon（Start time 8:30）) 9:00(Half Marathon（Start time 9:00-9:30）) 9:30(Half Marathon（Start time 9:30-10:00）) 10:00(Half Marathon（Start time 10:00-10:30）) 10:30(Half Marathon（Start time 10:30-11:00）) 13:00(5km run) 11:30(Parent-child run (500m))

info.Transportation Facility: 20 minutes by shuttle bus from Semine Station on the Tohoku Main Line. 30 minutes by shuttle bus from Kurikomakogen Station on the Tohoku Shinkansen Line.

notice: -Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.
-The race guide will be sent by email by the organizer in early April.
-For overseas runners, you can pick up your bibs on the race day at the Help Desk located around the T-shirts pick-up areas of the main venue, Naganuma Futopia Park.

-The full marathon starts at 8:30, and the half marathon can start at any time within the 30-minute time slot you specified when registering between 9:00 and 11:00. The parent-child run starts at 11:30, so if you are participating in both the half marathon and parent-child run, please start the half marathon early.
-No changes are possible once you complete your registration.

races.name: Sado Toki Marathon 2026
races.description: This marathon takes place on Sado Island, a remote island rich in nature where people and the internationally protected Japanese Crested Ibis coexist. The course is well known for its magnificent nature, including the blue ocean and snow-capped mountains, and the T-shirts are always a hit!

Event/Eligibility.Marathon【4/26】: Age 18 or older(Excludes high school students) Time Limit：7 hours *There is a check point.
Event/Eligibility.Half Marathon【4/26】: Age 18 or older(Excludes high school students) Time Limit：3 hours 30 minutes *There is a check point.
Event/Eligibility.10km【4/26】: Age 16 or older Time Limit：1 hour 40 minutes
Event/Eligibility.3km【4/25】: Age 6 or older Time Limit：30 minutes
Event/Eligibility.1.5km【4/25】: For all ages *Children 5 years old or younger must be accompanied by a parent or guardian who is 18 years or older. Time Limit：25 minutes

info.Participation Fee: Marathon: 7,500JPY, Half Marathon: 6,000JPY, 10km: 4,500JPY, 3km: 2,000JPY, 1.5km: 2,000JPY, 1.5kmParent-child 2,500JPY. *For a group of 2 persons. Additional 500JPY for each additional preschool child.
info.Check-in Time: [Full, Half, 10km] April 25 8:30 AM-4:00 PM, April 26 8:30 AM-10:00 AM [3km, 1.5km] April 25 8:30 AM-10:00 AM

Children 5 years old or younger must be accompanied by a parent or guardian who is 18 years or older.

races.name: 2026 UEDA VERTICAL RACE - The 12th Mt. Taro Mountain Climbing Race -
races.description: Japan's first urban mountain running race. The Sky Running Vertical and Sky Race will be held on Mt. Taro, beloved by the citizens of Ueda.  This year, the Ueda Sky Race (27 km in distance / 3100 m of cumulative elevation gain) will be held on May 4 as the 7 race of the Skyrunner World Series.

info.Organizers: Mt. Taro Mountain Climbing Race Executive Committee

Event/Eligibility.5.0km/+1000m Vertical SASUKE / May 3: Male and Female Age 12 or older
Event/Eligibility.3.5km/+700m Vertical YUKIMURA / May 3: Male and Female Age 6 or older
Event/Eligibility.3.5km/+700m Vertical TREKKING / May 3: Male and Female Age 6 or older

Event/Eligibility.3km/±300m Sky NINJA / May 4: Male and Female Age 12 or older

notice: Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.

You will be notified of your individual start time in advance. Please line up in the starting area 10 minutes before the start.

Participants under age 12 running the 3.5km Vertical YUKIMURA (Sanada Yukimura Course) can be accompanied by a parent or guardian. You will be asked to indicate your preference during the registration process, and if you have an accompanying runner, you will be asked to enter their name and relationship to the parent or guardian.

races.name: The 5th Mt. Fuji Sanroku Women's Trail Run
races.description: We support women who are newly challenging trail running in this women-only trail running race. Held at the foot of Mt. Fuji, as the Mt.FUJI100 Friendship Race, participants can enjoy the breathtaking views of Lake Kawaguchiko and Mt. Fuji. It's the perfect event for those making their trail running race debut.

info.Organizers: The General Incorporated Foundation R-bies Sports Foundation

Event/Eligibility.About 13km: Female aged 12 or older Time Limit：3hours

info.Participation Fee: Age 16 or older : 7,000 JPY Ages 12 to 15 : 3,000 JPY
info.Check-in Time: Day before the race May 9 2:00PM to 6:00PM, on the race day May 10 7:30AM to 9:30AM
info.Check-in Location: Day before the race, May 9 : Michi-no-Eki Katsuyama,on the race day, May 10 : Koumi Park
info.Check-in Address: Katsuyama, Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District, Yamanashi Prefecture
info.Award Ceremony: 1st to 3rd place, by age group, with special prizes
info.Transportation Facility: About 15minutes by taxi from Fujikyuko Line Kawaguchiko Station.

notice: - Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.
- The start will be a staggered start.
- This event uses the RUN PASSPORT smart check-in system.
Once your race bib exchange certificate (entry ticket) is ready, we will notify you at the email address you provided during registration.
Please download your entry ticket from the "Entry Ticket Issuance Notice" email, and either save it on your smartphone or print the PDF to bring with you to the event venue.
The email will be sent on April 30th.
＜Notice＞
*If any prohibited actions are discovered, the participant will be disqualified on the spot. When applying for the race, participants are required to agree to the following application regulations.
(1) The entire course is within the designated area of the "National Park" as specified by the Natural Parks Act for environmental conservation.
(2) Pedestrians have priority on the course, even during the competition. Please yield the path when encountering pedestrians.
(3) To protect the vegetation in the Mt. Fuji region, please thoroughly clean both racing and regular shoes. (Strict adherence to prevent the introduction of alien species' seeds, etc.)
(4) Damaging or causing destruction to the course, including forest roads, is strictly prohibited.
(5) Placing objects such as wood and soil on forest roads that obstruct passage is prohibited.
(6) Collection of plants, soil, and similar materials along the forest roads is prohibited.
(7) Parking on forest roads is prohibited.
(8) Absolutely do not leave water and food supplies waste on the course.
(9) Running off the trail is strictly forbidden.
(10) The use of poles or sticks is prohibited.
(11) It is recommended to carry the following items:
・Portable cup (for water)
・Bear bell

races.name: THE13TH RUNNET EKIDEN OKINAWA
races.description: This Ekiden Race, runners can run alongside the beach with refreshing feeling. A flat and easy-running course will welcome everyone to join!  Don’t be so hurry to go home soon after the race because the beach has a BBQ area.  It must be super fun to have a beachside party after finishing the race!

info.Organizers: General Incorporated Association R-bies Sports Foundation

Event/Eligibility.Solo 18km: Male and Female: Age 18 or older

info.Participant’s Prize: 【Solo】T-shirt or a pair of socks 【EKIDEN Walk 3km】drinks
info.Award Ceremony: Top 3 finishers of Male and Female （+ other special awards) *No awards for "EKIDEN Walk 3km."

notice: - Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.
- The Race Guide will be sent via email about 2 weeks before the race day.

- The course for this event is not reserved for participants (runners). Local cyclists and pedestrians may be sharing the same course during the race. Please be sure to pay great attention to running safely and give consideration to others.
- We set limit number of participants to ensure the safety of the course.
- Running alongside the participants by bicycle is prohibited for safety reasons.
-Wheelchair is not allowed in this race.
-【Solo】3 km × 6 laps
-If participants are under 18 years old, guardian's consent is required.

races.name: Nordisk Mountain Trail The 17th Fuji Oshino Kogen Trail Race
races.description: The Mt. FUJI100 Friendship Race Series 2026 will be held. With ample water stations and course guidance, this event is enjoyable for everyone from beginners to advanced runners. Participants will receive a T-shirt, pork miso soup or soba noodles after finishing, and a towel. Starting with the 2026 event, a new Family Run category will be introduced.

info.Organizers: Fuji Oshino Kogen Trail Race committee

Event/Eligibility.Approx. 35km: Male ＆Female, Ages 15 or older Time Limit：Final wave: 8 hours, with checkpoints
Event/Eligibility.Approx.21.6km: Male ＆Female, Ages 15 or older Time Limit：Final wave: 6 hours, with checkpoints
Event/Eligibility.Approx.13.2km: Male ＆Female, Ages 13 or older Time Limit：Final wave: 5 hours, with checkpoints
Event/Eligibility.Approx. 4km Family Run: Parent ages 18 or older with one child age 12 or younger Time Limit：50 minutes

info.Participation Fee: 35km: 7,000 JPY, 21.6km: 6,000 JPY, 13.2km: 5,000 JPY (Age13-15 3,500 JPY), Family Run 3,000 JPY
info.Check-in Time: The day before the event, from 2:00 PM to 5:00 PM, and on the day of the event, from 6:00 AM until 30 minutes before each start.
info.Check-in Address: 1666-36 Oshinomura Shimokusa, Minamitsuru-gun, Oshino village, Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan
info.Participant’s Prize: Original T-shirt, etc. Finisher's Award: Original towel, pork soup service, etc.※The participation prize for the Family Run is scheduled to be an original towel.
info.Award Ceremony: Overall 6th place for men and women in each event, plus 1st to 3rd place for men and women in each age group (under 29, 30s, 40s, 50s, 60+). ※Note: Winners placing overall within the top 6 are excluded from age group awards.※Family Run events do not include rankings or awards.

notice: -Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.

races.name: The 24th RISHIRI YU-YU-RUNNING
races.description: The 24th RISHIRI YU-YU-RUNNING event offers participants a unique opportunity to enjoy an extraordinary course that circles the island. Runners will be treated to breathtaking views, including the refreshing coastal scenery with the scent of the sea, the majestic Mt. Rishiri—one of Japan’s famed 100 mountains—and the picturesque sight of Hokkaido across the water.

Event/Eligibility.Approx. 53.67 km: Male/Female: 13 years old and above Time Limit：10 hours with checkpoints.

info.Participation Fee: Adults: 10,000 JPY, Ages 13-18 years old: 2,000 JPY
info.Check-in Time: 【 Only the day before (June 6) 】 9:00 AM - 7:00 PM
info.Participant’s Prize: Bib number, Kelp, Sweets, Water, Facial Mask, Hot Springs voucher
info.Award Ceremony: Awards will be given to the top 3 overall men and women and 1st finisher in each age group for men/women (junior high and high schools, 49 or under, 50s-60s, 70 or over).
info.Transportation Facility: Three ferry services are available between Wakkanai and Rishiri (15 minutes on foot from Oshidomari Port). One flight each between New Chitose and Rishiri (available from June 1) and between Okadama and Rishiri (10 minutes by bus from Rishiri Airport).

notice: - Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.

- Race Guide will be sent via email after the registration deadline of March 31. Please provide the email address where you would like to receive this information when applying. Registered participants can expect to receive their Race Guide in early April.
- Athlete check-in will only take place the day before the tournament. Race day check-in will not be available.
Date & Time: Saturday, June 6, 9:00 AM - 7:00 PM
Location: Rishiri-Fuji Town General Exchange Promotion Facility【Ripura】

- An Eve party (5,000 JPY) will be held for participants who have competed in the tournament 10 or more times. Qualifying individuals will be contacted directly by the tournament's office after registration.
・Eve party: 6:40 PM - 8:10 PM at Rishiri-Fuji Town Exchange Promotion Facility【Ripura】
- Parking space (in a nearby parking lot) will be provided only for those coming to the island by car.

Note: Accommodations near the event are limited, so we recommend reserving a place as early as possible. Flights to the area are also limited, so planning your travel arrangements in advance would be advisable.

races.name: The 23rd Fruit Kingdom Higashine Sakuranbo Marathon
races.description: Enjoy a refreshing run through Higashine City, Yamagata Prefecture, the number one cherry producing city in Japan and the birthplace of Sato Nishiki cherries! This is a marathon course where cherries shine Ruby ​​Red. The start and finish points are at the Japan Ground Self-Defense Forces, so you can run on the Self-Defense Force grounds. We will be providing GI Higashine Sato Nishiki cherries to all participants!

Event/Eligibility.Half Marathon: Male/Female: 19-29 years old, 30s, 40s, 50s, 60+ years old Time Limit：Total of 2hrs 50mins → Checkpoints at 12.8km (1hr 45mins), 16.4km (2hrs 15mins), and 18.7km (2hrs 32mins).
Event/Eligibility.10 km: Male/Female: 16-18 years old, 19-29 years old, 30s, 40s, 50s, 60+ years old Time Limit：Total of 1hr 35 mins → Checkpoints at 5.5 km (55 mins) and 7.6 km (1hr 15mins).
Event/Eligibility.5 km: Male/Female: 16-18 years old, 19-29 years old, 30s, 40s, 50s, 60+ years old Time Limit：Total of 50 mins → Checkpoint at 2.8 km (30 mins).
Event/Eligibility.3 km: Male/Female: 10-12, 13-15 years old, Parent-Child Division (Team of 2 or 3): Guardian (18 years old and above) + Child (7-9 years old) Time Limit：Total of 30 mins → No checkpoints.

races.name: Echigo Matsudai Harunojin Trail Run 2026
races.description: This is a unique rural trail race that features a collection of art installations from the Echigo-Tsumari Art Triennale (ETAT), beautiful terraced rice fields certified as "Connecting Terraced Heritage" by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries, and revitalized ancient paths. At the aid stations, you can enjoy local cuisine cultivated in snowy regions! After overcoming the harsh winter, experience the deep green of spring in Echigo Matsudai and the pristine landscapes of Japan. Please enjoy the race!

info.Organizers: Echigo Matsudai Trail Running Executive Committee

Event/Eligibility.25km Satoyama Trail Journey: Male and Female aged 15 or older, students (15-24 years old) *Minors require parental consent Time Limit：6hours 45minutes
Event/Eligibility.2km Junior & Family Trail Run: Male and female age (6-15), family pairs (children age 6-15 and parents over 18 years old)

info.Finish Line: NOHBUTAI Snow-Land Agrarian Culture Center, Matsudai

races.name: ECHIGO COUNTRY TRAIL
races.description: Meeting the local people and learning about the local lifestyle and flavors. This is truly a journey. This race will give you the kind of experience that will make you want to live in Niigata.

Event/Eligibility.63km: Age 16 or older Time Limit：11 hours 30 minutes*There are cutoff points.
Event/Eligibility.22km: Age 13 or older Time Limit：5 hours *There are cutoff points.
